keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Observability & Diagnostics platform engineered to a network diagnostics dashboard designed to run reachability analyses, track packet flows, and verify connectivity paths..
Integrates Network Intelligence Center capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the Enterprise Networking and Security Architecture portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Establishes globally routing, logically isolated networks spanning multiple Google Cloud regions privately.
Guarantees highly available hybrid connectivity to on-premises sites using redundant VPN tunnels or private fiber paths.
Balances user traffic dynamically at Google Cloud's edge, directing requests to the lowest-latency healthy backend instance.
Aggregates system metrics, application logs, and request trace spans into centralized dashboards.
Supports alert routing, threshold alarms, and log filtering to diagnose system health anomalies in real-time.
Monitors audit logs to ensure compliance and trace administrative actions across Google Cloud resources.
Governs access to Network Intelligence Center using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/networkintelligencecenter.admin and roles/networkintelligencecenter.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Network Intelligence Center that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Network Intelligence Center resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Network Intelligence Center operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Network Intelligence Center resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Network Intelligence Center resource
commands.command:
gcloud compute networks create prod-networkintelligencecenter-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Network Intelligence Center Resource"

commands.description: List active Network Intelligence Center instances and query configuration states
commands.command: gcloud compute networks list --format="table(name, state, updateTime)"
